mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.0% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.25 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 102.0 signals stronger manufacturing activity that improves corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO > 102.0, or the predictive power of INDPRO declines to an IC below 0.10 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust aggregate demand, creating positive momentum in equity markets that persists for 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or the interaction term's coefficient drops below 0.05 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in residential sales (RSAFS) above 625,000 units signals stronger consumer spending confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 55%+ of instances where RSAFS > 625000, or the predictive IC of RSAFS falls below 0.15 in 24-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 280) during a monetary tightening cycle reduces real bond yields, creating a negative feedback loop that depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of CPIAUCSL > 280 and FEDFUNDS > 0.1% fails to predict negative SPX returns in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) paired with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market that drives equity valuations higher through improved earnings prospects over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of UNRATE decline and PAYEMS growth on SPX returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or the interaction term's coefficient drops below 0.07 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 101.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 55%+ of instances where INDPRO < 101.0, or the predictive IC of INDPRO falls below -0.12 in 36-month rolling windows.
